description: Cross-port numeric conformance fixtures for arrayop contraction (ess-zmm). Executing ports must simulate each fixture and assert that every inline 'tests' assertion matches within the declared tolerance. Non-executing ports explicitly skip with a documented reason. See README.md for the skip contract and adapter notes.

skip_bindings.typescript: TypeScript binding has no arrayop numeric executor; round-trip only.
skip_bindings.go: Go binding has no arrayop numeric executor; round-trip only.

fixtures.notes: Julia executes via both the tree-walk path (build_evaluator generalized-einsum unroll, tree_walk.jl:294-370) and the MTK path (EarthSciSerializationMTKExt _build_arrayop_sym). The tree-walk path requires the LHS-arrayop form; the MTK path additionally handles the embedded form. Fixture 23 uses the LHS-arrayop form, which both Julia paths support.

skip_bindings.typescript: TypeScript binding has no arrayop numeric executor.
skip_bindings.go: Go binding has no arrayop numeric executor.

skip_bindings.typescript: TypeScript binding has no arrayop numeric executor.
skip_bindings.go: Go binding has no arrayop numeric executor.

skip_bindings.typescript: TypeScript binding has no arrayop numeric executor; round-trip only.
skip_bindings.go: Go binding has no arrayop numeric executor; round-trip only.

fixtures.notes: Pins _resolve_index_of_makearray (ESS tree_walk.jl:1114) in the LHS-arrayop context: the RHS body is index(makearray(regions,values),i,j) where i,j are the arrayop output loop variables. The LHS-arrayop unroller substitutes concrete ints for i,j via _sub_preserving, then _resolve_indices calls the resolver. 3x3 2D domain with two overlapping regions (border=1.0, center=4.0). Python uses the scalar fallback in _eval_arrayop. Rust uses ctx.loop_binds in eval_arrayop/eval_index. (ess-e9l)

skip_bindings.julia: Julia tree-walk (build_evaluator) rejects arrayop in expression position (E_TREEWALK_UNSUPPORTED_OP). Julia MTK path may handle scalar reduce via _arrayop_scalar_reduce but is not yet validated for the embedded-RHS form; tracked as ess-n0w.
skip_bindings.typescript: TypeScript binding has no arrayop numeric executor.
skip_bindings.go: Go binding has no arrayop numeric executor.

fixtures.notes: Exercises the embedded contraction form: arrayop appears as the RHS of a scalar D equation, not as the LHS-arrayop form. This is the form that ess-n0w adds support for in the Julia tree-walk path.
